articles.title: Millions fill Tehran for main day of Khamenei's funeral as new supreme leader stays out of sight
articles.summary: The largest crowds of the week were expected as the main funeral procession for slain Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei began at 6am on July 6, following a 10km route from Imam Hossein Square to Azadi Square, with airspace over Tehran fully closed. Mourners carried red 'revenge' flags and chanted 'Death to America' and 'Death to Israel'. New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, Ali's son, has still not appeared publicly and his whereabouts remain unknown, fuelling questions over who is leading Iran. Burial is set for July 9 at the Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad, after processions in Qom, Najaf and Karbala.

articles.title: Trump says Iran got 'a week off' for funeral and is 'dying to settle' as talks pause
articles.summary: With US-Iran negotiations paused for the week of funeral ceremonies, President Trump cast the lull as a favour, saying 'We gave them a week off for a funeral because we're nice,' and declared Iran is 'dying to settle.' Trump pledged calm during the mourning period. The pause follows the June 17 memorandum of understanding that extended the ceasefire by 60 days; the next round of indirect talks, mediated by Qatar, is expected after the funeral, while Netanyahu has signalled he is ready to resume strikes on Iran.

articles.title: Whereabouts of Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei remain unknown as mourning peaks
articles.summary: Even as huge crowds filled Tehran for the funeral, Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has yet to make a public appearance since his father's killing on February 28, communicating only through written statements shared by state media. Mojtaba is thought to have been wounded in the same US-Israeli strike that killed his father, mother and wife, and has remained in hiding since the war began in late February, deepening doubts about his health and control of the country.

articles.title: Hezbollah and Hamas delegations attend Khamenei funeral ceremonies in Tehran
articles.summary: Delegations from Iran-backed Hezbollah and Hamas were among the foreign and allied groups attending the funeral ceremonies for Ali Khamenei, alongside dozens of official foreign delegations. Their presence underscored Tehran's continued leadership of the 'axis of resistance' even after months of war, and drew criticism from Israeli officials who pointed to the attendance of designated militant groups.

articles.title: IDF chief tours captured Beaufort area, says army ready to resume offensive if Lebanon truce breaks
articles.summary: Israel's military chief toured Beaufort Castle and a nearby seized Hezbollah tunnel in south Lebanon, saying the IDF is prepared to renew offensive operations if the framework agreement is violated. Lebanese and Israeli media reported an Israeli strike sending up smoke near the southern village of Nabatieh al-Faouqa, days after Israel, Lebanon and the US signed the July 1 trilateral framework aimed at disarming Hezbollah, which its leader Naim Qassem has rejected as 'humiliating.'

articles.title: Netanyahu rejects Vance's claim Trump is Israel's only friend, vows Iran will never get a nuclear weapon
articles.summary: In a Fox News interview, PM Netanyahu pushed back on Vice President JD Vance's claim that Trump is Israel's only ally, insisting Israel has 'many friends' while stressing its unique bond with Washington. He said that even if the US fails to reach a nuclear deal with Tehran, he will ensure Iran never obtains a nuclear weapon while he is prime minister, and claimed some Christian villages in Lebanon had 'asked to be annexed to Israel' for protection from Hezbollah.

articles.title: Gaza's ceasefire passes 1,000-day mark with bodies unclaimed and reconstruction stalled
articles.summary: Nearly nine months after the October 11 ceasefire, Gaza remains in a state of 'neither war nor peace': the UN says at least 1,059 people have been killed since the deal was signed, governance handover to a Palestinian technocratic committee has not begun, and the proposed international stabilisation force has not materialised. Israel controls some 70% of the enclave beyond the 'yellow line'. A UN Security Council review this month is expected to confirm the disarmament impasse at the heart of the peace plan, as Gaza's media office reports the war's cumulative toll above 73,000 dead.

articles.title: Saudi, Bahraini foreign ministers stress freedom of navigation in the Gulf amid US-Iran talks
articles.summary: Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan met his Bahraini counterpart Abdullatif bin Rashid Al Zayani in Riyadh, where the two stressed the importance of freedom of navigation in the Arabian Gulf and regional stability, and reviewed the US-Iran negotiation track. The meeting came as commercial traffic in the Strait of Hormuz shows signs of recovery under the June ceasefire, even as Iran insists it will charge vessels 'service fees' and Washington says it will not permit tolls in any final deal.

articles.title: Israeli settlers burn down West Bank restaurant near Nablus, causing $300,000 in damage
articles.summary: A group of Israeli settlers broke into a restaurant near Al-Zaytouna University between Al-Lubban ash-Sharqiya and Ammuriya, south of Nablus, stole money, smashed its doors and set it ablaze, destroying it with losses estimated at about $300,000. Middle East Monitor said the attack took place under Israeli army protection amid a sharp rise in settler assaults across the occupied West Bank, where Palestinian data record 1,175 killed since October 2023.

articles.title: Israeli army detains settler activists who tried to breach the border with Syria
articles.summary: The Israeli military said it detained a group of settler activists who attempted to breach the frontier with Syria, part of a wider pattern of nationalist activists testing borders and settlement lines. The incident came as Israel maintains an expanded security presence in southern Syria following the collapse of the Assad government.

articles.title: Israeli government says it won't heed High Court broadcast regulator ruling, raising constitutional crisis fears
articles.summary: In what critics warned could become a constitutional crisis, the Israeli government said it will not comply with a High Court of Justice ruling concerning the country's broadcast regulator, setting up a fresh confrontation between the Netanyahu coalition and the judiciary. The stance drew condemnation from opposition figures amid continued anti-government demonstrations.

articles.title: Settlers attack Palestinian homes in Sinjil, north of Ramallah
articles.summary: Israeli settlers, accompanied by livestock, attacked areas surrounding Palestinian homes in the northern part of Sinjil, north of Ramallah, the latest in a string of settler assaults reported across the occupied West Bank on Sunday. Residents said the incursions are part of a broader effort to seize agricultural land and pressure families to leave.

articles.title: With Hormuz reopened, analysts ask whether the oil shortage has turned into a glut
articles.summary: As shipping through the Strait of Hormuz continues to stabilise under the 60-day US-Iran ceasefire, analysts say the market has swung from war-driven shortage fears toward the prospect of a glut, with a temporary opening for Iranian oil exports. Both assumptions remain fragile: traffic has not returned to pre-war levels because of security concerns, sea mines and the unresolved dispute over Iranian 'service fees'.
